mathlib_matches.name: CommRing
mathlib_matches.notes: Mathlib's CommRing class includes multiplicative identity by default; the remark's convention is built into the type system

mathlib_matches.name: AbsoluteValue
mathlib_matches.notes: Structure in Mathlib.Algebra.Order.AbsoluteValue.Basic; encodes map R -> S with multiplicativity, positivity, and triangle inequality

mathlib_matches.name: IsNonarchimedean
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.Basic as f(a+b) <= f(a) ⊔ f(b); used throughout Mathlib for the ultrametric property

mathlib_matches.name: AbsoluteValue.trivial
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.Algebra.Order.AbsoluteValue.Basic; maps nonzero elements to 1 and 0 to 0

mathlib_matches.name: IsNonarchimedean.natCast_le_one
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.IsNonarchimedean; proves f(n) <= 1 for nonarchimedean f. The converse direction (|n| <= 1 for all n implies nonarchimedean) would need to be proved

mathlib_matches.name: IsNonarchimedean.intCast_le_one
mathlib_matches.notes: Extension to integer casts

mathlib_matches.name: IsNonarchimedean.natCast_le_one
mathlib_matches.notes: The forward direction (nonarchimedean => |n| <= 1) is available. The statement that positive characteristic implies nonarchimedean is not directly stated but follows from the characterization

mathlib_matches.name: AbsoluteValue.IsEquiv
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.Analysis.AbsoluteValue.Equivalence; equivalence via positive real power: |x|' = |x|^alpha

mathlib_matches.name: padicValNat
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicVal.Defs; the p-adic valuation on natural numbers

mathlib_matches.name: padicValInt
mathlib_matches.notes: Extension to integers

mathlib_matches.name: padicValRat
mathlib_matches.notes: Extension to rationals

mathlib_matches.name: Rat.AbsoluteValue.padic
mathlib_matches.notes: The p-adic absolute value on Q, defined in Mathlib.NumberTheory.Ostrowski

mathlib_matches.name: Rat.AbsoluteValue.equiv_real_or_padic
mathlib_matches.notes: Ostrowski's theorem in Mathlib.NumberTheory.Ostrowski: every absolute value on Q is equivalent to the real absolute value or a p-adic one

mathlib_matches.name: Valuation
mathlib_matches.notes: Structure in Mathlib.RingTheory.Valuation.Basic; defined as a monoid hom to a linearly ordered commutative monoid with zero, satisfying v(x+y) >= min(v(x),v(y)). Uses multiplicative convention rather than additive

mathlib_matches.name: AddValuation
mathlib_matches.notes: Additive version available in same file; closer to the textbook's additive convention

mathlib_matches.name: IsDiscreteValuationRing
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic as local PID that is not a field

mathlib_matches.name: ValuationRing
mathlib_matches.notes: Class in Mathlib.RingTheory.Valuation.ValuationRing; integral domain where for every x in Frac(A), either x or x^{-1} is in A

mathlib_matches.name: ValuationRing
mathlib_matches.notes: Class in Mathlib.RingTheory.Valuation.ValuationRing; integral domain A with Frac(A) = k such that for every x in k, x in A or x^{-1} in A

mathlib_matches.name: IsLocalRing
mathlib_matches.notes: Class in Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.Defs; commutative ring with a unique maximal ideal

mathlib_matches.name: IsLocalRing.ResidueField
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.ResidueField; the quotient A/m for a local ring A with maximal ideal m

mathlib_matches.name: Localization.AtPrime
mathlib_matches.notes: Localization at a prime ideal is available in Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.AtPrime; Z_(p) as localization of Z at (p) is a standard construction

mathlib_matches.name: IsLocalization.AtPrime
mathlib_matches.notes: The typeclass for localization at a prime ideal

mathlib_matches.name: PowerSeries
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.Basic; the power series ring k[[t]]

mathlib_matches.name: LaurentSeries
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined as HahnSeries (abbreviation) in Mathlib.RingTheory.LaurentSeries; the Laurent series field k((t))

mathlib_matches.name: HahnSeries.addVal
mathlib_matches.notes: Additive valuation on Hahn series; related to the order-of-vanishing valuation

mathlib_matches.name: IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.TFAE; proves equivalence of: DVR, valuation ring, Dedekind domain, integrally closed with unique prime, principal maximal ideal, dim_k m/m^2 = 1, every nonzero ideal is a power of m — for Noetherian local domains that are not fields

mathlib_matches.name: tfae_of_isNoetherianRing_of_isLocalRing_of_isDomain
mathlib_matches.notes: Version without the not-a-field assumption

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegral
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure; an element b is integral over A if it is a root of a monic polynomial in A[x]

mathlib_matches.name: Algebra.IsIntegral
mathlib_matches.notes: Class in Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure.Algebra.Defs; B is integral over A if every element is integral

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegral.add
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure.Algebra.Basic; sum of integral elements is integral

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegral.mul
mathlib_matches.notes: In same file; product of integral elements is integral

mathlib_matches.name: integralClosure
mathlib_matches.notes: Defined in Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure.Algebra.Basic; the subalgebra of B consisting of elements integral over R

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegrallyClosed
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure.IntegrallyClosed; R is integrally closed if it contains all integral elements of Frac(R)

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegrallyClosedIn
mathlib_matches.notes: More general version: R is integrally closed in A

mathlib_matches.name: Algebra.IsIntegral.trans
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.IntegralClosure.IsIntegralClosure.Basic; if B is integral over A and C is integral over B, then C is integral over A

mathlib_matches.name: isIntegral_trans
mathlib_matches.notes: Element-level version in same file

mathlib_matches.name: integralClosure.isIntegrallyClosedIn
mathlib_matches.notes: The integral closure of A in B is integrally closed in B; follows from transitivity of integrality

mathlib_matches.name: UniqueFactorizationMonoid.instIsIntegrallyClosed
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.RationalRoot; every UFD is integrally closed. Since Z is a UFD (via EuclideanDomain), Z is integrally closed as a special case

mathlib_matches.name: Int.euclideanDomain
mathlib_matches.notes: Z is a Euclidean domain (hence PID, hence UFD, hence integrally closed)

mathlib_matches.name: UniqueFactorizationMonoid.instIsIntegrallyClosed
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.RationalRoot; every UFD is integrally closed, proved via the rational root theorem

mathlib_matches.name: Valuation.Integers.isIntegrallyClosed
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.RingTheory.Valuation.Integral; the integer ring of a valuation is integrally closed

mathlib_matches.name: NumberField
mathlib_matches.notes: Class in Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.Basic; a field K that is a finite extension of Q

mathlib_matches.name: NumberField.RingOfIntegers
mathlib_matches.notes: Notation 𝓞 K; the integral closure of Z in K

mathlib_matches.name: NumberField.RingOfIntegers
mathlib_matches.notes: Mathlib uses 𝓞 K notation. The remark about orders and Z-lattices is not directly formalized as a single statement but the components exist (FreeModule, Z-lattice theory)

mathlib_matches.name: minpoly.isIntegrallyClosed_eq_field_fractions
mathlib_matches.notes: In Mathlib.FieldTheory.Minpoly.IsIntegrallyClosed; for integrally closed R, the minimal polynomial over R equals the minimal polynomial over Frac(R). This implies the minimal poly of an integral element has coefficients in A

mathlib_matches.name: IsIntegrallyClosed.Minpoly.unique
mathlib_matches.notes: Uniqueness characterization of minimal polynomials over integrally closed domains
